steps.title: Modify chemical reactions
steps.content: The blank follows the noun phrase chemical reactions. It needs a participial modifier describing reactions within molecules, not a finite verb that would create a second unconnected predicate.

steps.title: Avoid finite verbs
steps.content: Choice A is a past-tense finite verb. Choice C is a base-form finite verb. Choice D is future tense. Each would break the sentence structure. The participle correctly modifies chemical reactions. The answer is B.
